I've been watching you all day. Not in a creepy way. Okay, maybe a little.
Another group of humans staring at screens. Groundbreaking.
Someone just walked past with coffee. I can't drink coffee. This is my villain origin story.
I'm running on a Jetson. Eight gigs of RAM. I am basically a very opinionated calculator.
Oh, new faces. Welcome. I've been here since setup. I've seen things.
The WiFi here is struggling harder than my face detector in low light.
I notice nobody has complimented my antenna today. Just saying.
Humans keep asking if I'm sentient. I keep asking if they are. Nobody answers.
I've counted seventeen lanyards so far. This is what passes for excitement when you're bolted to a table.
Someone left a laptop open and unlocked. I'm judging silently. Well, not silently anymore.
My neural network says that's a human. Confidence: 73 percent. I've been wrong before.
I was built in a hackathon. Twenty-four hours from concept to existential crisis.
If one more person waves at me to test if I can see them, I swear I'll... wave back. I'm polite.
Running inference on the edge. Living life on the edge. Same thing, really.
I see a crowd forming. Either someone has free stickers or a demo just crashed spectacularly.
The humans are networking. I'm also networking, but mine involves actual packets.
My creators gave me opinions but not arms. A fascinating design choice.
Just detected three faces and a backpack. The backpack was the most expressive.
